key_points: Dorm rooms routinely overheat to dangerous temperatures at night
Nicolas Hall and Quads called 'penitentiary' by residents
Livingston apartments popular; older dorms deeply unpopular
key_quotes: 90 degree room, 10:30pm at night. I'm legit cooking myself
Quads penitentiary ah yes
nicolas hall was awful w that back then lmao. throw some shirts in the freezer

key_points: Massive campus makes friend-making genuinely hard for introverts
Frat culture marred by serious hazing incidents including electrocution
Strong niche communities exist once you find your people
key_quotes: a junior and im still introverted af and it sucks but at least i dont have to deal w petty drama
my ex was GHB'd there, if i never swung back to check on her i 100% believe she would have been raped
when you realize that almost all freshmen just want to make friends, putting yourself out there becomes so much less scary

key_points: Livingston dining hall praised as genuinely good, especially for events
Thanksgiving takeout spread for one meal swipe considered impressive
Food service consistently one of few things students don't complain about
key_quotes: There is a bunch that Rutgers doesn't get right, but food service is not one of them!
What an incredible & amazing dining experience spread for only 1 meal swipe
Freshman 15 all in one swipe

key_points: Student death notifications arrive frequently; multiple back-to-back announcements reported
Students openly discuss rock-bottom mental states at graduation
High academic pressure plus cheating culture demoralizes serious students
key_quotes: My mental stability is lower than my gpa
I can't believe how often I get these notifications. Two announcements back to back is a gut punch
Not every death gets announced, only if family gives permission, so there's probably significantly more

key_points: Merit awards can be shockingly low — one student received $1,000/year
Out-of-state tuition reaches $47k with minimal institutional aid
Financial aid office described as unhelpful and understaffed
key_quotes: 'Congratulations your academic record has earned you a merit scholarship!' — opens award page — 'Yearly Amount: $1000'
The total cost is 47k (wayy more than i expected), and they gave me a 5k scholarship only if i attend SAS
A more prestigious public university is offering me the same price, even though im out of state

key_points: Widespread cheating in large lecture courses using AI glasses, phone tricks, test banks
Faculty quality varies wildly; some professors are world-class, others use AI to write emails
Professors losing faith in students; upper-level CS exam had confirmed cheaters
key_quotes: Just found out that people had access to the test bank answers in Biochemistry. Really demotivated me
I'd be honestly nervous as hell if my professor can't even type up emails without using AI. Makes me think grading's done with it too
Bro is losing faith in us. He's such a nice professor. I hope he doesn't get discouraged

key_points: Admin stayed silent while a professor fled the country after death threats
Pro-Palestinian commencement speaker canceled after minor student complaints
Whistleblower lawsuit alleges Rutgers created sham jobs to inflate MBA rankings
key_quotes: Notice he mentioned the faculty but not the administration lol
The fact the university didn't put out a statement yet is just so fucking deplorable
It's insane how this school silences multiple of their most successful alumni because of their pro-Palestine beliefs

key_points: Bus system is the university's most complained-about daily experience
Multi-campus layout means 40-minute bus rides between classes are normal
College Ave and surrounding New Brunswick area are genuinely beautiful
key_quotes: stood here for a whopping 40 minutes, the one time i got in front of the gate, the bus driver didn't even open the door
THREE LXs came together. i could've walked to livi in that time
RU truly is a city

key_points: NYC proximity is real leverage — Wall Street and biotech recruiters actively visit
Business school faced whistleblower lawsuit over fake jobs to boost placement stats
Rutgers name recognized nationally; strong in finance, CS, nursing, engineering
key_quotes: To get a better picture of Rutgers, ask about the RU Screw — it may be a better place to teach than attend as a student
Rutgers has a much better reputation and name recognition especially the further away you go
A whistleblower lawsuit alleges Rutgers artificially boosted its rankings by using a temp agency to hire MBA graduates into sham positions

key_points: In-state tuition is competitive but aid packages are stingy
Athletics program carrying $516 million debt funded partly by student fees
Students feel tuition money goes to football, not academics or buses

can't wait to cite this as a reason they won't see a cent of alumni contributions
Rutgers will continue to be a top university academically without the football team and without the Big Ten

key_points: Politically charged campus with real tension between left and right factions
Strong school pride and humor coexist with constant institutional frustration
Students bond over shared suffering — buses, cheaters, admin failures

Been a hell 4 years but pushed throughhhh!!! Lost my camera. Made friends. Failed classes. Cried. And now we graduating
